content.full_text:
September 10, 1992

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir/Madam:

I recently spoke to a member of the FPPC legal staff requesting a reading on whether or not we would need to file a report if we provide subscriptions to legislators without cost. The response was the suggestion that we write a letter describing what we do, etc. and request the FPPC to inform us in writing if we are lobbying or if we "fall under anything in the Act."

We, the Workers' Comp Executive, are a news organization. We are not lobbyists or a political action group. We publish a twice monthly newsletter, the Workers' Comp Executive, duly recognized as Capitol Press Corps members. Our subscriptions cost $395 per year. The principal subscribers of the newsletter are insurance company executives, the State Fund, insurance agents and brokers and risk managers of major corporations in California.

We want to send copies of our newsletter on a regular basis, at no charge, to selected elected officials or candidates, generally those whom we cover or who might be involved in insurance issues.

We are not attempting in any way to influence legislators, the voters or anyone else, but rather to make certain that those we cover know who we are since we are not a large publication like the Sacramento Bee. No candidate, official, or lobbyist has or will ever influence what we cover or how we cover the news.

The cost of providing the newsletter to each individual is less than $2 per person per month.

Enclosed are copies of the last three issues. Please advise me on any reporting requirements that affect us.

Sincerely,

John P. McCann
Editor-in-Chief

Encl. 3 WCE: Vol. 2, Nos. 15, 16, 17
